channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org

item.title: Competing and Intertwined Orders in Boson-Doped Mott Antiferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Xin Lu, Jia-Xin Zhang, Lukas Homeier, Shou-Shu Gong, D. N. Sheng, and Zheng-Yu Weng

Inspired by the recent experimental advances in cold atom quantum simulators, we explore the experimentally implemented bosonic $t–{t}^{′}–J$ model on the square lattice using large-scale density matrix renormalization group simulations. By tuning the doping level $δ$ and hopping ratio ${t}^{′}/t$, …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096506] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xin Lu, Jia-Xin Zhang, Lukas Homeier, Shou-Shu Gong, D. N. Sheng, and Zheng-Yu Weng

Inspired by the recent experimental advances in cold atom quantum simulators, we explore the experimentally implemented bosonic t–t′–J model on the square lattice using large-scale density matrix renormalization group simulations. By tuning the doping level δ and hopping ratio t′/t, we uncover six d…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096506] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Competing and Intertwined Orders in Boson-Doped Mott Antiferromagnets
item.dc:creator: Xin Lu, Jia-Xin Zhang, Lukas Homeier, Shou-Shu Gong, D. N. Sheng, and Zheng-Yu Weng
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Magnetic-Flux Tuning of Second Harmonic Content in Intrinsic ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ Josephson Junction
item.description:
Author(s): Jing-Jing Chen, Han-Xin Lou, Xing-Guo Ye, Zhen-Bing Tan, An-Qi Wang, Zhen-Tao Zhang, Yu-Xuan Wang, Xin Liao, Zhen-Sheng Zhang, Zhi-Min Liao, and Da-Peng Yu

The current phase relation (CPR) determines the electromagnetic response of Josephson junctions in superconducting circuits. It is sensitive to the weak link materials and interface quality. In this work, we report the intrinsic Josephson effect originating from domains in the single-crystal kagome …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096301] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jing-Jing Chen, Han-Xin Lou, Xing-Guo Ye, Zhen-Bing Tan, An-Qi Wang, Zhen-Tao Zhang, Yu-Xuan Wang, Xin Liao, Zhen-Sheng Zhang, Zhi-Min Liao, and Da-Peng Yu

The current phase relation (CPR) determines the electromagnetic response of Josephson junctions in superconducting circuits. It is sensitive to the weak link materials and interface quality. In this work, we report the intrinsic Josephson effect originating from domains in the single-crystal kagome …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096301] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic-Flux Tuning of Second Harmonic Content in Intrinsic ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ Josephson Junction
item.dc:creator: Jing-Jing Chen, Han-Xin Lou, Xing-Guo Ye, Zhen-Bing Tan, An-Qi Wang, Zhen-Tao Zhang, Yu-Xuan Wang, Xin Liao, Zhen-Sheng Zhang, Zhi-Min Liao, and Da-Peng Yu

item.title: Electric-Field-Tuned Consecutive Topological Phase Transitions between Distinct Correlated Insulators in Moiré ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ Heterobilayer
item.description:
Author(s): Xumin Chang, Zui Tao, Bowen Shen, Wanghao Tian, Jenny Hu, Kateryna Pistunova, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Tony F. Heinz, Tingxin Li, Kin Fai Mak, Jie Shan, and Shengwei Jiang

Consecutive topological phase transitions (TPTs) between strongly correlated electronic phases that differ simultaneously in symmetry breaking and topological order are of fundamental interest in condensed matter physics, yet, rarely realized experimentally. We report two consecutive electric-field–…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096503] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xumin Chang, Zui Tao, Bowen Shen, Wanghao Tian, Jenny Hu, Kateryna Pistunova, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Tony F. Heinz, Tingxin Li, Kin Fai Mak, Jie Shan, and Shengwei Jiang

Consecutive topological phase transitions (TPTs) between strongly correlated electronic phases that differ simultaneously in symmetry breaking and topological order are of fundamental interest in condensed matter physics, yet, rarely realized experimentally. We report two consecutive electric-field–…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096503] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Electric-Field-Tuned Consecutive Topological Phase Transitions between Distinct Correlated Insulators in Moiré ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ Heterobilayer
item.dc:creator: Xumin Chang, Zui Tao, Bowen Shen, Wanghao Tian, Jenny Hu, Kateryna Pistunova, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Tony F. Heinz, Tingxin Li, Kin Fai Mak, Jie Shan, and Shengwei Jiang

item.title: Orbital-Selective Spin-Orbit Mott Insulator in Fractional Valence Iridate ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ir}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{11}$
item.description:
Author(s): Kai Wang, Jun Yang, Chaoyang Kang, Weikang Wu, Wenka Zhu, Jianzhou Zhao, Yaomin Dai, and Bing Xu

The combination of strong spin-orbit coupling and Coulomb interactions makes the $5d$ iridates a unique platform for realizing novel correlated electronic states. Here, utilizing infrared spectroscopy, we demonstrate that a robust Mott insulating state persists in the $1/3$-hole self-doped system ${…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096501] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kai Wang, Jun Yang, Chaoyang Kang, Weikang Wu, Wenka Zhu, Jianzhou Zhao, Yaomin Dai, and Bing Xu

The combination of strong spin-orbit coupling and Coulomb interactions makes the 5d iridates a unique platform for realizing novel correlated electronic states. Here, utilizing infrared spectroscopy, we demonstrate that a robust Mott insulating state persists in the 1/3-hole self-doped system La3Ir3…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096501] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Orbital-Selective Spin-Orbit Mott Insulator in Fractional Valence Iridate ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ir}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{11}$
item.dc:creator: Kai Wang, Jun Yang, Chaoyang Kang, Weikang Wu, Wenka Zhu, Jianzhou Zhao, Yaomin Dai, and Bing Xu

item.title: Kinetic Energy Driven Ferromagnetic Insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Jinyuan Ye, Yuchi He, and Congjun Wu

We construct a minimal model of interacting fermions establishing a ferromagnetic insulating phase. It is based on the Hubbard model on a trimerized triangular lattice in the regime of $t≫|{t}^{′}|>0$ with $t$ and ${t}^{′}$ the intra- and intertrimer hopping amplitudes, respectively. At the $\fra…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086504] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jinyuan Ye, Yuchi He, and Congjun Wu

We construct a minimal model of interacting fermions establishing a ferromagnetic insulating phase. It is based on the Hubbard model on a trimerized triangular lattice in the regime of t≫|t′|>0 with t and t′ the intra- and intertrimer hopping amplitudes, respectively. At the 13-filling, each trim…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086504] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Kinetic Energy Driven Ferromagnetic Insulator
item.dc:creator: Jinyuan Ye, Yuchi He, and Congjun Wu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Topological Chiral Superconductivity in the Triangular-Lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard Model
item.description:
Author(s): Feng Chen, Wen O. Wang, Jia-Xin Zhang, Leon Balents, and D. N. Sheng

Moiré materials provide exciting platforms for studying the interplay of strong electronic correlation and large magnetic flux effects. We study the lightly doped Hofstadter-Hubbard model on a triangular lattice through the large-scale density matrix renormalization group and determinantal quantum M…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086503] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Feng Chen, Wen O. Wang, Jia-Xin Zhang, Leon Balents, and D. N. Sheng

Moiré materials provide exciting platforms for studying the interplay of strong electronic correlation and large magnetic flux effects. We study the lightly doped Hofstadter-Hubbard model on a triangular lattice through the large-scale density matrix renormalization group and determinantal quantum M…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086503] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological Chiral Superconductivity in the Triangular-Lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard Model
item.dc:creator: Feng Chen, Wen O. Wang, Jia-Xin Zhang, Leon Balents, and D. N. Sheng

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Bilayer Graphene Quantum Dots as a Quantum Simulator of Haldane Topological Quantum Matter
item.description:
Author(s): Daniel Miravet, Hassan Allami, Marek Korkusiński, and Paweł Hawrylak

We demonstrate here that a chain of bilayer graphene quantum dots (BLGQDs) can realize topological quantum matter by effectively simulating a spin-1 chain that hosts the Haldane phase within a specific range of parameters. We describe a chain of BLGQDs with two electrons per dot using an atomistic t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086604] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Daniel Miravet, Hassan Allami, Marek Korkusiński, and Paweł Hawrylak

We demonstrate here that a chain of bilayer graphene quantum dots (BLGQDs) can realize topological quantum matter by effectively simulating a spin-1 chain that hosts the Haldane phase within a specific range of parameters. We describe a chain of BLGQDs with two electrons per dot using an atomistic t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086604] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Bilayer Graphene Quantum Dots as a Quantum Simulator of Haldane Topological Quantum Matter
item.dc:creator: Daniel Miravet, Hassan Allami, Marek Korkusiński, and Paweł Hawrylak
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Manipulating Charge Distribution in Moiré Superlattices by Light
item.description:
Author(s): Ruiping Guo, Haowei Chen, Wenhui Duan, Yong Xu, and Chong Wang

Uniform optical illumination is predicted to induce static but spatially inhomogeneous charge redistribution within a supercell in moiré superlattices.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086903] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ruiping Guo, Haowei Chen, Wenhui Duan, Yong Xu, and Chong Wang

Uniform optical illumination is predicted to induce static but spatially inhomogeneous charge redistribution within a supercell in moiré superlattices.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086903] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Manipulating Charge Distribution in Moiré Superlattices by Light
item.dc:creator: Ruiping Guo, Haowei Chen, Wenhui Duan, Yong Xu, and Chong Wang
